title: LinkedIn is spying on you, according to a new 'BrowserGate' security report scripts stealthily scan visitors' browsers for over 6,000 Chrome extensions and harvest hardware data
summary: LinkedIn injects a JavaScript script on every page load to scan visitors' browsers for installed Chrome extensions and collect device telemetry, potentially compromising users' privacy.
insight: LinkedIn's BrowserGate script violates users' trust and online security.

title: McDonald s debuts one-finger gadget that lets you move your character to 'keep you in the game' while you chow down Pro Gamer Menu's Archie designed to keep you from being kicked for inactivity
summary: McDonald's Turkey introduces a one-finger gadget to prevent gamers from getting kicked for inactivity during meal breaks. This gadget allows users to maintain their online presence without manually moving their character. The 'Pro Gamer Menu's Archie' is designed to keep gamers online.
insight: Convenience trumps inconvenience for gamers during meal breaks

title: Nvidia AI tech claims to slash gaming GPU memory usage by 85% with zero quality loss Neural Texture Compression demo reveals stunning visual parity between 6.5GB of VRAM and 970MB
summary: Nvidia's Neural Texture Compression (NTC) technique reduces VRAM usage by 85% with zero quality loss, utilizing a neural network to decompress textures and improve image quality.
insight: Massive VRAM reduction enables more powerful gaming on lower-end hardware.

title: Iranian missile blitz takes down AWS data centers in Bahrain and Dubai Amazon reportedly declares hard down status for multiple zones
summary: Iranian missile strikes reportedly took down AWS data centers in Bahrain and Dubai, leading to a 'hard down' status for multiple zones at Amazon Web Services.
insight: Global cloud infrastructure vulnerability exposed by regional conflict escalation.

title: Sharge Disk Pro 2TB review: Great sustained writes, active cooling, and a built-in hub
summary: The Sharge Disk Pro 2TB boasts a 10 Gbps SSD, active cooling, and a built-in port hub, making it a compact, feature-rich solution for gamers and road warriors, but at a high price.
insight: Balances performance, convenience, and portability in a premium package

title: Hands-on with Modos Tech 13.3-inch e-paper monitors we tried the current Dev Kit model and the next-gen Modos Flow touch
summary: Modos Tech sent us their latest e-paper monitors for hands-on testing. We tried the current Dev Kit model and the next-gen Modos Flow touch screen model. Both units provided insight into potential future display technologies.
insight: Next-gen e-paper displays could revolutionize screen usage habits.

title: Modder uses Claude AI to rewrite BIOS so they can boot unsupported 12 P-core Bartlett Lake CPU in Windows on a Z790 motherboard
summary: A modder has successfully booted an unsupported 12-core Bartlett Lake-S CPU into Windows using a custom BIOS modification on an Asus Z790 motherboard. This breakthrough demonstrates the potential for modders to unlock new CPU capabilities on existing hardware. The achievement could pave the way for future upgrades and customizations.
insight: Modding enables CPU upgrades beyond manufacturer's supported specifications.

title: Enermax Revolution III S 1000W power supply review: Platinum efficiency, limited thermal capability
summary: The Enermax Revolution III S 1000W offers certified Platinum efficiency and good power quality, but is limited by a small cooling fan and peculiar cable layout.
insight: Limited thermal capability affects long-term reliability significantly.

title: Microsoft to force updates to Windows 11 25H2 for PCs with older Windows 11 OS versions 'intelligent' update system uses machine learning to determine when a device is ready
summary: Microsoft is upgrading Windows 11 24H2 systems to 25H2 automatically, streamlining updates before support ends.
insight: Streamlined updates ensure Windows 11 PCs remain secure and supported.

title: 'Every Microsoft engineer got a stopwatch,' says Windows veteran reminiscing about company's past focus on speed asserts that 'everything' was timed to ensure acceptable performance in the 1980s
summary: A former Microsoft Windows Division president reminisces about the 1980s coding regime where everything was timed to ensure acceptable performance, highlighting the company's focus on speed and efficiency during this period.
insight: Focus on speed led to efficient coding and performance optimization.

title: Enthusiast installs Win 3.1X on bare metal Ryzen 9 9900X and RTX 5060 Ti system using floppy disk drive, OS from 1992 running on 2025 hardware Asus motherboard s classic BIOS functionality was instrumental to the feat
summary: A retro computing enthusiast successfully installed and booted Windows 3.1X on a high-end Ryzen 9 9900X and RTX 5060 Ti PC system using a floppy disk drive, showcasing the versatility of classic BIOS functionality.
insight: Legacy BIOS capabilities can bridge vast technological gaps.

title: New 'GeForge' and 'GDDRHammer' attacks can fully infiltrate your system through Nvidia's GPU memory Rowhammer attacks in GPUs force bit flips in protected VRAM regions to gain read/write access
summary: New 'GeForge' and 'GDDRHammer' attacks target Nvidia's GPU memory, causing bit flips in VRAM to gain read/write access. These attacks bypass protected regions by manipulating page files and directories. This allows attackers to access CPU memory.
insight: Vulnerable GPU memory allows attackers to breach CPU security boundaries

title: Researchers 3D print robot the size of a single-cell organism devices move and navigate even without a brain, uses their shape and the environment to get going
summary: Researchers at Leiden University created 0.5-micrometer microrobots that move and navigate using shape and environment.
insight: Paving the way for brainless robots in micro-scale applications.

title: ESP32-S3 gets post-quantum encryption with Aethyr Edge Node open-source firmware
summary: Aethyr Research has released open-source firmware for ESP32-S3 with post-quantum encryption, using ML-KEM-768 and other secure algorithms. This firmware boots in 2.1 seconds and supports full PQC handshakes in 35ms. It is designed to provide quantum-resistant security for IoT applications.
insight: Preparing for quantum computers that can crack traditional encryption methods.

title: This Wi-Fi receiver can work inside a nuclear reactor, keeping robots connected
summary: A Wi-Fi receiver can operate in high-radiation environments, like nuclear reactors. It surpasses the endurance of space-grade electronics. This makes it suitable for extreme settings.
insight: Radiation-resistant Wi-Fi technology enables robotics in extreme environments.

title: Steam could soon show estimated FPS based on crowd-sourced player data
summary: Steam may display estimated FPS based on player data, improving PC gaming performance expectations.
insight: Actual in-game performance often diverges sharply from recommended hardware specs.

title: New Rowhammer attack can grant kernel-level control on Nvidia workstation GPUs
summary: Researchers have found a new Rowhammer attack on Nvidia GPUs, granting kernel-level access to Linux systems. The vulnerability is more severe than previously thought, affecting GPUs based on Nvidia's Ampere and Ada Lovelace architectures. This attack exploits GDDR6-based vulnerabilities.
insight: Kernel-level control allows for severe privilege escalation and data theft.

title: Microsoft's AI in its own terms: "use Copilot at your own risk"
summary: Microsoft's Copilot terms of use warn users of potential errors and inaccuracies. The software is intended for entertainment purposes only, and users are advised against relying on it. This emphasizes the limitations of AI technology.
insight: AI limitations must be acknowledged and respected by users always

title: Visa processed 106 million disputes last year, now wants AI to take over
summary: Visa processed 106 million disputes last year, now wants AI to streamline the process and cut losses.
insight: Efficiency gains through AI could save Visa significant time and financial losses.

title: US military deploys low-cost "Lucas" drone built from reverse-engineered Iranian tech
summary: The US military has deployed low-cost 'Lucas' drones built from Iranian tech, used against Iranian targets, resulting in an 83% decline in Iranian drone attacks.
insight: US adapts and counter-attacks Iranian drone threats with reverse-engineered tech.

title: Nearly half of US data centers planned for 2026 are facing delays or cancellation
summary: Up to 50% of US AI data centers face delays or cancellation due to equipment and power constraints.
insight: US data center delays impact AI development and future innovation capabilities
